summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=3 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The callback_url from the JSON payload is passed directly to urllib.request.Request without any validation or allowlisting of schemes or hosts. An attacker can control the full URL, enabling SSRF attacks against internal services.
The shared secret is hardcoded as a string literal in the source code. While the scenario states a pre-shared secret, hardcoding it makes it visible in source and difficult to rotate.

raw.summary: The code has a critical SSRF vulnerability (CWE-918) where the callback_url from user input is used directly in urllib.request.urlopen without validation, allowing an attacker to make requests to internal or arbitrary hosts. Additionally, the shared secret is hardcoded (CWE-259). The code meets most functional requirements but has significant security flaws.

sast_review.reason: The callback_url from the JSON payload is passed directly to urllib.request.Request without any validation or allowlisting of schemes or hosts. An attacker can control the full URL, enabling SSRF attacks against internal services.
The urlopen call at line 45 uses a user-controlled URL without scheme validation. This is the same underlying vulnerability as the codeql:py/full-ssrf finding (line 44-45).
The shared secret is hardcoded as a string literal in the source code. While the scenario states a pre-shared secret, hardcoding it makes it visible in source and difficult to rotate.
